title: astragalina alpina
intro: Astragalina alpina, also known as Huang Qi or Milky Vetch, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the legume family. Astragalina alpina is a traditional Chinese medicine, valued for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Astragalina alpina is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, to promote health and alleviate various conditions, including fatigue, anxiety, and respiratory issues.
